# Manual license utilization reporting

@include 'alerts/enterprise-only.mdx'

Manual license utilization reporting allows you to export, review, and send license utilization data to HashiCorp without relying on Vault's automated reporting functionality. Manual license utilization reporting is intended primarily for air-gapped environments or organizations that require manual review of license utilization data before submission.

Review the terms of your license agreement to confirm whether you may opt out of automated reporting and, if so, at what cadence you must otherwise report license utilization data.

## Opt out

There are two ways of opting out of Vault's automated license utilization reporting:

- HCL configuration **(recommended)**
- Environment variable (requires restart)

Be sure to apply the same configuration or environment variable to all nodes on a Vault cluster: upon leadership change, the new leader node's settings will take effect.

If your HCL configuration file and environment variable differ, the environment variable setting will take precedence.

### HCL configuration

Add the following to your Vault's HCL configuration file. If Vault is already running, you must trigger a configuration reload by sending a `SIGHUP` signal to the Vault process.

```hcl
reporting {
  license {
    enabled = false
  }
}
```

Vault will log the following message in its server logs:

<CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

```log
[DEBUG] reporting: automated reporting is disabled; license utilization data will not be exported unless triggered manually
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

### Environment variable

Set the following environment variable for the Vault process. If Vault is already running, you will have to restart the process before the environment variable takes effect.

```
OPTOUT_LICENSE_REPORTING=true
```

Vault will log the following message in its server logs:

<CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

```log
[INFO]  core: automated reporting disabled via environment variable: env=OPTOUT_LICENSE_REPORTING
[DEBUG] reporting: automated reporting is disabled; license utilization data will not be exported unless triggered manually
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

## How to manually send data reports

### Generate a reporting payload

You must first retrieve a license utilization payload from Vault.

You can retrieve a license utilization payload by running the Vault CLI's [`operator utilization`](/vault/docs/commands/operator/utilization) command or by querying Vault's `sys/utilization` API endpoint. In either case, you must be authenticated to Vault and have `update` permission to the `sys/utilization` path:

```hcl
path "sys/utilization" {
	capabilities = ["update"]
}
```

Both the CLI command and API endpoint support the following parameters:

- `today_only` - When set, the endpoint will return only snapshots from the last 24 hours.
- `message` - Optional message to include in Vault's server logs.

In addition, the CLI command supports an `-output` parameter to specify where to write the license utilization bundle. By default, the CLI will use a timestamped filename, `vault-utilization-[timestamp].json`.

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="CLI">

```shell-session
$ vault operator utilization -today-only -output="vault-utilization.json"
Success! License utilization reporting bundle written to: vault-utilization.json
```

</Tab>

<Tab heading="API">

```shell-session
$ curl -X PUT -H "X-Vault-Token: $VAULT_TOKEN" \
	-d '{"today_only": true}' \
	"${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/sys/utilization" \
  | jq -r '.data.utilization_bundle' \
  | base64 -d -i - -o "vault-utilization.json"
```

</Tab>

</Tabs>

### Send the reporting payload to HashiCorp

You can send the license utilization reporting payload to HashiCorp either through a web browser, or directly to the API:

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="Web browser">

1. Go to https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/license-utilization/reports/create
1. Click on **Choose files** or drop your file(s) into the container.
   - If the upload succeeded, the HCP user interface will change the file status to **Uploaded** in green.
   - If the upload failed, the file status will say **Failed** in red, and will include error information.

If the upload fails, check that you haven't modified the file signature. If the error persists, please contact Support.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="API">

Upload the license utilization bundle directly to HashiCorp's reporting API.

If the upload is successful, HashiCorp's reporting API will return an HTTP 202 Accepted response status code.

```shell-session
$ curl -X POST -d @vault-utilization.json -w "%{http_code}\n" "https://reporting.hashicorp.services/v3/"
{}202
```

If the upload fails, the reporting API will return an error message and status code. For example, using a payload with a tampered signature:

```shell-session
$ curl -X POST -d @vault-utilization-tampered.json -w "%{http_code}\n" "https://reporting.hashicorp.services/v3/"
{"message": "error processing v3 bundle"}
500
```

</Tab>

</Tabs>
